fields.summary: Specifies the color primaries of a color space as defined by the CICP (Coding-Independent Code Points) standard ITU-T H.273.
fields.remarks:
<format type="text/markdown"><![CDATA[
## Remarks

`SKColorspacePrimariesCicp` enumerates the standardized color primary sets from
ITU-T H.273. Each member corresponds to the numeric code point assigned by that
standard. Pass a value of this enum alongside a
<xref:SkiaSharp.SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp> to
<xref:SkiaSharp.SKColorSpace.CreateCicp*> to construct an
<xref:SkiaSharp.SKColorSpace> from CICP metadata embedded in image files (for
example, AVIF or HEIC).

## Examples

```csharp
// Create an sRGB-equivalent color space using CICP codes
var colorSpace = SKColorSpace.CreateCicp(
    SKColorspacePrimariesCicp.Rec709,
    SKColorspaceTransferFnCicp.Iec6196621);
```
]]></format>

fields.summary: Generic film color primaries (CICP code point 8).
ITU-T H.273 code point 22, used for EBU Tech 3213-E color primaries.
ITU-R BT.2020 / BT.2100 color primaries, used for ultra-high-definition television (CICP code point 9).
ITU-R BT.470 System B/G color primaries, used for PAL and SECAM standard definition television (CICP code point 5).
ITU-R BT.470 System M color primaries, used for NTSC standard definition television (CICP code point 4).
ITU-R BT.601 color primaries, used for standard definition television (CICP code point 6).
ITU-R BT.709 color primaries, used for high-definition television and sRGB displays (CICP code point 1).
SMPTE EG 432-1 (P3-D65) color primaries, used for digital cinema and Apple Display P3 (CICP code point 12).
SMPTE RP 431-2 (P3-DCI) color primaries, used for digital cinema projection (CICP code point 11).
SMPTE ST 240 color primaries (CICP code point 7).
SMPTE ST 428-1 (XYZ) color primaries, used for digital cinema (CICP code point 10).
Unknown or unspecified color primaries (CICP code point 0).
